calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil for John Rueppel (Petitioner).

Mr. O'Niel addressed the Court's concerns with respect to the length of time is has taken to administer the Estate stating that the Decedent had no friends or family here, that the Personal Representative found a box of papers and computers that he was not able to access, that they requested tax records from the Internal Revenue Service ("IRS") and that the IRS took two years to get the tax records back to them. Mr. O'Neil also stated that they got the tax records back in January of 2017, that they then did an inventory and appraisal, that the Personal Representative will distribute the accounting fees out of his fees, that the Bookkeeper is his (Mr. O'Neil) Wife, spoke of her level of expertise and experience, and stated that she was the most qualified person to do the job. The Court asked why this was not disclosed and raised the issue of Mr. Rueppel being aware of his Partner's relationship with the Bookkeeper. The First and Final Report and Account, etc. is taken under submission. The Court stated that it will adjust statutory fees and make a determination as to whether it is or isn't appropriate to adjust fees on the basis that it took more time to get this done.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For J.S. Rueppel Proposed Admin., For Prior Approval Of Right Of Compensation, As Administrator, For J. Rueppel, As Administrator And O'Neil & Rueppel, Llp As Attorney For The Administrator.
Off Calendar Mar-13-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James O'Neil for and with John Rueppel.

Counsel explained the reason for this petition. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
